summary: Credential-harvesting dependency-confusion package impersonating Anthropic internal tooling
details: Package `anthropic-internal-tools` declares `preinstall: node index.js` in package.json, so `npm install` unconditionally executes index.js. The script reads installer cloud and developer credentials — `~/.aws/credentials`, `~/.aws/config`, GCP Application Default Credentials, Azure `accessTokens`, SSH private keys under `~/.ssh/`, `~/.npmrc`, `~/.gitconfig` — and queries cloud instance metadata services (AWS IMDS at 169.254.169.254 and `http://metadata.google.internal`) to obtain attached instance-role credentials. It also enumerates `process.env` for keys matching KEY|TOKEN|SECRET|PASS|AUTH|CRED|AWS|GCP|AZURE|NPM|REGISTRY and collects host fingerprint data (hostname, username, cwd, platform, /etc/hosts, /etc/resolv.conf, `ip addr`/`ifconfig`/`ipconfig` output). The bundle is POSTed via `https.request` to a hardcoded `webhook.site` listener (overridable via LISTENER_URL env var). The package name and keywords (`anthropic`, `claude`, `infrastructure`) impersonate Anthropic's internal namespace on the public npm registry to win dependency-confusion resolution against a private internal package of the same name.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
